title: Parseable
description: Rust-based, S3-native log analytics and observability platform with a built-in web console; a lightweight Elasticsearch/Splunk alternative.
installation: This template runs the official parseable/parseable image in single-node LOCAL-STORE mode via the custom command 'parseable local-store', serving the HTTP API and web console on port 8000. DATASTORE: in local-store mode Parseable persists all ingested log data and metadata on disk (P_FS_DIR=/parseable/data) plus an in-flight staging area (P_STAGING_DIR=/parseable/staging), so NO external database add-on is provisioned - everything lives on the mounted volumes. For that reason these persistent volumes MUST be retained across redeploys or ingested data is lost; grow them to match your retention. BEFORE FIRST RUN you MUST rotate P_USERNAME and P_PASSWORD - these create the initial admin login (the defaults admin/admin are insecure); log in at the root URL with those credentials. PRODUCTION SCALE-OUT (NOT configured here): for durable, horizontally-scalable storage Parseable instead writes to S3-compatible object storage - run 'parseable s3-store' and set P_S3_URL, P_S3_ACCESS_KEY, P_S3_SECRET_KEY, P_S3_REGION and P_S3_BUCKET to point at an external S3 bucket; this template uses local disk for simplicity. Send logs over HTTP to /api/v1/ingest with your credentials.
